serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Stir-Fried Flower-Shaped Pork Kidney as a shared side dish at a Chinese meal is approximately 150 g, representing one to two portions of the scored and stir-fried kidney pieces.

nutrient_source.title: 宫廷抓炒腰花 Stir-Fried Flower-Shaped Pork Kidney — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 宫廷抓炒腰花 Stir-Fried Flower-Shaped Pork Kidney (cr_id G00010147). All per-100g values are as reported. Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry and is set to -1. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, water, and micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Stir-Fried Flower-Shaped Pork Kidney. The recorded carbohydrate_g is 0.0 per 100g per HPB; however, the dish contains cornstarch batter and sweet sauce in preparation, contributing some glycaemic load in practice. A conservative analogous GI of 55 is assigned based on cornstarch-battered and sauce-coated stir-fried dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 150g serving is calculated as (55 × 0.0 × 1.5) / 100 = 0.0 based on reported carbohydrate; actual GL may be higher due to unrecorded batter starch. GL is reported as 0.0 per the HPB-recorded carbohydrate value with this caveat noted.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg per 100 g — far exceeding the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold — driven by soy sauce and dark soy sauce in the preparation
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this dish or request a significantly reduced-soy-sauce version; a 150 g serving delivers approximately 1575 mg sodium, representing over 68% of the 2300 mg daily limit in a single dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Cornstarch batter and sweet sauce contribute refined starch and added sugar not fully captured in the recorded 0 g carbohydrate figure, raising actual glycaemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the HPB-recorded carbohydrate of 0 g/100 g likely underestimates the true glycaemic impact of the cornstarch batter and sweet sauce; portion control and requesting minimal batter and reduced-sugar sauce are advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1050 mg/100 g), cholesterol (89 mg/100 g), and deep-fried preparation with pork offal elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this dish due to the combination of very high sodium, moderate dietary cholesterol from pork kidney, and the pro-inflammatory effect of deep-frying; if consumed, restrict to a small portion infrequently.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and pork offal delivers arachidonic acid, both amplifying inflammatory pathways; high sodium worsens joint swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish; the combination of deep-fried pork offal, high sodium, and purine-rich kidney is contraindicated for RA management and may worsen comorbid gout.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-Fried Flower-Shaped Pork Kidney (宫廷抓炒腰花, Gōngtíng Zhuā Chǎo Yāo Huā) is a classic Chinese palace-style dish in which pork kidney is scored in a cross-hatch flower pattern, coated in a light cornstarch batter, deep-fried until crisp, and then tossed in a glossy sweet-and-sour sauce made from soy sauce, dark soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic. The scoring technique causes the kidney pieces to curl into decorative flower shapes during cooking, giving the dish its name. The texture is crisp on the outside with a tender, slightly chewy interior, and the flavour profile is savoury, tangy, and mildly sweet. Per 100 g, the dish provides 171 kcal, 19.1 g protein, 10.5 g fat, and negligible recorded carbohydrate, though the actual glycaemic contribution from the batter and sauce is higher than the recorded figure suggests. Sodium is very high at 1050 mg/100 g, making this dish unsuitable for patients with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Cholesterol is 89 mg/100 g. The dish is a popular banquet and restaurant item in Singapore's Chinese cuisine repertoire, typically served as a shared side dish at lunch or dinner. Clinically, the very high sodium, deep-fried preparation, and pork offal content make this dish high-concern for hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis patients.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010147), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. The match is exact. Confidence is high.
notes: carbohydrate_g is recorded as 0.0 per the HPB source; this is a known limitation of the HPB entry — the dish uses cornstarch batter and a sweet sauce in preparation, so the actual carbohydrate and glycaemic load are likely higher than recorded. Confirmed zero per HPB record; value retained as reported by the approved source rather than estimated upward without a cited source. sugar_g is 0.0 per HPB — confirmed zero per HPB record; actual sugar from sauce is likely non-zero but not separately quantified in the approved source. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 per HPB — naturally zero for a meat-based dish with minimal vegetable content. saturated_fat_g set to -1: not reported by HPB for this entry; detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific dish as of 2026-04-16. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: fatty acid fractions not reported by HPB for this entry. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported by HPB for this entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail not reported by HPB for this specific dish entry as of 2026-04-16. GI assigned as analogous estimate (55, low) based on cornstarch-battered stir-fried dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database; actual GI measurement for this specific dish is not available.
